improvements: The comparison view now shows a dedicated card layout on mobile instead of a cramped horizontal table, making it much easier to read results on a phone
A TL;DR summary bar appears at the top of the mobile comparison view, letting you see all net salaries and effective rates at a glance and tap to scroll to any country card
When comparing countries with different currencies, net salaries are now shown in a common base currency alongside the original local amount, so you can make apples-to-apples comparisons
Salary sync across destinations now fetches all currency conversions in a single batch instead of one request per country, making updates faster when you type a new gross salary
Exchange rate lookups are now cached in the browser for one hour and de-duplicated, so switching between currencies does not trigger redundant network requests
The add-destination wizard form fields now stack to a single column on small screens, preventing cramped two-column layouts on narrow devices
The sticky row-label column in the desktop comparison table now has a subtle shadow to visually separate it from the scrollable data columns
